content_summary: This report analyzes fiscal dominance in Brazil—the phenomenon where deteriorating public finances undermine the central bank's ability to control inflation. It explains the concept through coordination problems between monetary and fiscal authorities, examines Brazil's current position (finding active monetary policy but concerning fiscal trajectory), and assesses market dynamics indicating growing fiscal risk despite inflation targeting framework remaining functional.
